location.description: PostgreSQL connection URI: postgres://[user[:password]@]host[:port][/database]. If a database path is included, it is used as the target for pg_restore when restoring single-database dumps.

host.description: PostgreSQL server hostname (default: localhost). Overrides the host in the location URI.

port.description: PostgreSQL server port (default: 5432). Overrides the port in the location URI.

username.description: PostgreSQL username. Overrides the username in the location URI.

password.description: PostgreSQL password. Overrides the password in the location URI.

database.description: Target database for pg_restore when restoring a single-database dump. If omitted, the database name is inferred from the dump filename. Not used when restoring a full pg_dumpall dump (all.sql).

create_db.description: When true, passes -C to pg_restore so the target database is created from the archive metadata. The database parameter (or postgres) is used only as the initial connection database. Defaults to false.

restore_globals.description: When true, feeds globals.sql (roles and tablespaces) to psql before restoring the database dump. Only relevant for single-database backups; ignored for pg_dumpall restores which already include globals. Defaults to false.

no_owner.description: When true, passes --no-owner to pg_restore so that ALTER OWNER commands are skipped. Useful when the roles from the source server do not exist on the target server.

exit_on_error.description: When true, passes -e to pg_restore and ON_ERROR_STOP=1 to psql so restore stops on the first error. Defaults to false, matching pg_restore/psql behaviour.

schema_only.description: When true, passes -s to pg_restore to restore only schema (no data). Mutually exclusive with data_only. Not applicable to pg_dumpall restores.

data_only.description: When true, passes -a to pg_restore to restore only data (no schema). Mutually exclusive with schema_only. Not applicable to pg_dumpall restores.

pg_bin_dir.description: Directory containing the PostgreSQL client binaries (pg_restore, psql). When omitted, binaries are resolved via $PATH.

ssl_mode.description: SSL mode for the connection, passed via PGSSLMODE (default: prefer).

ssl_cert.description: Path to the client SSL certificate file (PEM format), passed via PGSSLCERT.

ssl_key.description: Path to the client SSL private key file (PEM format), passed via PGSSLKEY.

ssl_root_cert.description: Path to the root CA certificate used to verify the server (PEM format), passed via PGSSLROOTCERT.
